title: Yara and the Missing Banana Mystery
synopsis: When the zoo's prize banana goes missing, young detective Yara teams up with her animal friends to solve the mystery. Following clues through the zoo, they discover the surprising truth about who took the banana and why.

pages.text: Yara loved visiting Sunny Grove Zoo. She had curly black hair and always wore her bright yellow detective hat. Today was special. The zoo was showing off their biggest banana ever! It was as long as a baseball bat and bright yellow. 'This banana is from our prize tree,' said Zookeeper Maria. She had kind brown eyes and long braided hair. 'We've been growing it for six months!' All the animals were excited. Benny the monkey clapped his hands. Ella the elephant trumpeted happily. Even Ollie the owl hooted with joy.

illustration.description: A young girl with curly black hair wearing a bright yellow detective hat stands next to a tall woman with long braided hair in a zoo setting. They're looking at an enormous yellow banana displayed on a pedestal. Colorful zoo animals including a monkey, elephant, and owl are gathered around looking excited. Bright sunny day with lush green trees and colorful flowers.
illustration.alt: Yara in her yellow detective hat and Zookeeper Maria admiring a giant banana while zoo animals watch excitedly

pages.text: But the next morning, something terrible happened. 'OH NO!' cried Zookeeper Maria. 'The banana is GONE!' Yara gasped. The pedestal was empty! Only a few yellow crumbs were left behind. 'This is a mystery,' said Yara. She pushed up her detective hat. 'Don't worry, Maria. I'll find your banana!' Benny the monkey swung down from his tree. 'I'll help!' he chattered. 'Me too!' trumpeted Ella the elephant. Ollie the owl flapped his wings. 'Whoo wants to solve a mystery?' All the animals wanted to help Detective Yara!

illustration.description: Yara pointing at an empty pedestal with yellow crumbs scattered around it while Zookeeper Maria looks distressed with her hands on her cheeks. The monkey, elephant, and owl are gathered around looking determined to help. The scene has dramatic lighting with shadows to show the mystery mood, but still bright and colorful.
illustration.alt: Yara and the animals discovering the empty pedestal where the giant banana used to be

pages.text: Yara looked carefully at the empty pedestal. 'Let's look for clues,' she said. She got out her magnifying glass. It had a bright red handle. 'I see yellow crumbs,' said Yara. 'And look! Tiny footprints!' The footprints were very small. Much smaller than elephant feet. Smaller than monkey paws too. 'These prints are round,' said Ella. 'Like little circles.' Benny scratched his head. 'What animal has round feet?' Ollie hooted thoughtfully. 'This is getting interesting!' Yara wrote everything down in her detective notebook. Yellow crumbs. Tiny round footprints. The mystery was just beginning!

illustration.description: Yara crouched down examining tiny round footprints with a red-handled magnifying glass while the monkey, elephant, and owl peer over her shoulders. Yellow banana crumbs are scattered on the ground. The scene shows close-up detail of the clues with warm golden lighting highlighting the evidence.
illustration.alt: Detective Yara examining clues with her magnifying glass while her animal friends help investigate

pages.text: The tiny footprints led away from the pedestal. 'Follow that trail!' said Yara. The friends walked slowly. They didn't want to miss any clues. The prints went past the monkey house. They went around the elephant pool. They went under the owl tree. 'Where are they going?' wondered Benny. The trail led to the petting zoo area. There were goats and sheep there. There was also Carlos, a boy with glasses who loved to feed the animals. 'Hi Carlos!' called Yara. 'Did you see anything strange this morning?'

illustration.description: Yara leading a parade of animals following tiny footprints that wind through different zoo areas. The monkey swings from branch to branch, the elephant follows behind, and the owl flies overhead. Carlos, a boy with glasses, waves from the petting zoo area where goats and sheep can be seen. Bright, cheerful zoo setting with winding paths.
illustration.alt: Yara and her animal friends following the mysterious footprint trail through the zoo

pages.text: Carlos pushed his glasses up his nose. 'I did see something!' he said. 'This morning, I saw little animals running. They were carrying something yellow!' 'What kind of little animals?' asked Yara. She got ready to write in her notebook. 'They were round and furry,' said Carlos. 'With tiny legs and wiggly whiskers!' Ella flapped her big ears. 'That sounds like...' 'Guinea pigs!' hooted Ollie. 'Of course!' said Yara. 'Guinea pigs have round feet!' The footprints made sense now. But why would guinea pigs take the banana?

illustration.description: Carlos, a boy with glasses, gesturing excitedly while talking to Yara and the animals. In a thought bubble above his head, small round furry guinea pigs are shown carrying pieces of yellow banana. The petting zoo is in the background with goats and sheep. Everyone looks surprised and excited by this revelation.
illustration.alt: Carlos explaining what he saw to Detective Yara and her animal friends

pages.text: The footprint trail kept going. It led past the petting zoo to a quiet corner. There was a small building there. It had a sign that said 'Small Animal House.' 'I've never been here before,' said Yara. Benny jumped up and down. 'Me neither!' The building was painted light blue with white trim. There were small windows and a red door. The tiny footprints went right up to the door! 'This must be where the guinea pigs live,' said Ella. Yara took a deep breath. 'Let's go inside and solve this mystery!' She knocked gently on the red door. Knock, knock, knock!

illustration.description: A small, charming blue building with white trim and a red door, surrounded by flowers and bushes. Yara stands at the door with her hand raised to knock while the monkey, elephant, and owl gather behind her. The tiny footprint trail is clearly visible leading right to the door. Cozy, inviting atmosphere with dappled sunlight.
illustration.alt: Yara and the animals arriving at the Small Animal House following the footprint trail

pages.text: The red door opened slowly. A small guinea pig peeked out. She had brown and white fur and looked very worried. 'Oh no!' she squeaked. 'We're in trouble, aren't we?' More guinea pigs appeared. There was a black one, a orange one, and a gray one with spots. 'I'm Yara,' said the detective kindly. 'I'm looking for a missing banana. Can you help me?' The guinea pigs looked at each other. The brown and white one sighed. 'My name is Rosie,' she said. 'We... we took the banana. But we had a good reason!' 'Tell me about it,' said Yara. She sat down to listen.

illustration.description: Several adorable guinea pigs with different colored fur (brown and white, black, orange, gray with spots) peeking out from a red doorway looking worried. Yara sits cross-legged in front of them with a kind, understanding expression. The monkey, elephant, and owl are gathered around listening. Soft, warm lighting creates a cozy, safe feeling.
illustration.alt: Guinea pigs confessing to Detective Yara while her animal friends listen

pages.text: Rosie the guinea pig spoke in her tiny voice. 'Yesterday was Pip's birthday!' She pointed to the smallest guinea pig. He was white with brown patches. 'Pip has been sick,' said Rosie. 'He couldn't eat regular food. The vet said he needed special soft food.' The other guinea pigs nodded sadly. 'We heard about the big banana,' said the black guinea pig. 'Bananas are soft! Perfect for Pip!' 'We took it to help our friend,' squeaked the orange one. Yara's eyes got wide. 'You took the banana to help Pip feel better?' 'Yes,' said all the guinea pigs together.

illustration.description: The guinea pigs gathered around tiny Pip, who looks small and fragile with white fur and brown patches. The other guinea pigs look caring and protective. Yara's expression shows understanding and sympathy. In the background, pieces of the yellow banana can be seen, prepared in small, soft pieces perfect for a sick guinea pig. Warm, emotional lighting.
illustration.alt: The guinea pigs explaining how they took the banana to help sick little Pip

pages.text: Yara looked at little Pip. He did look much better now! His eyes were brighter. His fur was fluffy. He even smiled at her! 'You know what?' said Yara. 'I think you did the right thing.' The guinea pigs gasped. 'Really?' squeaked Rosie. 'Really,' said Yara. 'But next time, you should ask for help first.' Benny clapped his hands. 'That's what friends do!' Ella nodded her big head. 'We all want to help each other!' Ollie hooted happily. 'Whoo knew guinea pigs were so caring!' Yara stood up. 'Come on, everyone. Let's go talk to Zookeeper Maria!'

illustration.description: Little Pip looking much healthier and happier, surrounded by his guinea pig friends who look relieved. Yara smiles warmly while the monkey claps, the elephant looks pleased, and the owl spreads his wings happily. Everyone appears joyful and united. Bright, celebratory lighting with warm golden tones throughout the scene.
illustration.alt: Everyone celebrating that Pip is feeling better and the mystery is solved

pages.text: Back at the main zoo, Yara found Zookeeper Maria. She was still looking sad about the missing banana. 'Maria!' called Yara. 'I solved the mystery!' She told Maria the whole story. About the guinea pigs. About sick little Pip. About how they just wanted to help their friend. Maria's eyes filled with happy tears. 'Oh my!' she said. 'Those sweet guinea pigs!' 'They're sorry they took the banana without asking,' said Yara. 'But they're not sorry they helped Pip!' Maria laughed. 'I'm not sorry either! That's exactly what friends should do!'

illustration.description: Yara explaining the story to Zookeeper Maria, who has tears of joy in her eyes and her hands clasped together. The guinea pigs are visible in the background looking hopeful. The monkey, elephant, and owl stand proudly with Yara. Bright, uplifting zoo setting with sunshine streaming through trees.
illustration.alt: Yara telling Zookeeper Maria the heartwarming truth about the missing banana

pages.text: Maria had a wonderful idea. 'Let's have a party!' she announced. 'A birthday party for Pip!' All the animals cheered. Maria brought out a new banana. Not as big as the prize one, but perfect for sharing. She cut it into pieces for everyone. The guinea pigs got the softest pieces. Pip ate happily. His friends watched with joy. Carlos came with some lettuce from the petting zoo. Even more zoo visitors joined the celebration! 'This is the best mystery ever!' said Yara. She was right. Sometimes the best mysteries have the happiest endings.

illustration.description: A joyful birthday party scene with all the characters gathered around picnic tables. Colorful balloons and streamers decorate the area. Everyone is sharing banana pieces and other treats. Pip sits in the center wearing a tiny party hat, looking happy and healthy. Zoo visitors of different ages and backgrounds have joined the celebration. Festive, warm, celebratory atmosphere.
illustration.alt: A wonderful birthday party for Pip with all the zoo animals and visitors celebrating together

pages.text: As the sun set over Sunny Grove Zoo, Yara put away her detective hat. 'Good work today, Detective Yara!' said Benny. 'You solved the mystery!' said Ella. 'And made everyone happy!' hooted Ollie. Yara hugged her animal friends. She waved goodbye to the guinea pigs. Rosie called out, 'Thank you for understanding!' Little Pip squeaked a tiny 'Thank you too!' Zookeeper Maria walked Yara to the zoo entrance. 'Will you come back soon?' she asked. Yara grinned. 'Of course! You never know when there might be another mystery!' She skipped home, already excited for her next adventure at Sunny Grove Zoo.

illustration.description: A beautiful sunset scene at the zoo entrance with Yara waving goodbye to all her friends. The golden sunset light bathes everything in warm colors. The animals are gathered together waving back, with the guinea pigs visible in the distance. Zookeeper Maria stands beside Yara. Peaceful, heartwarming ending with purple and orange sunset sky.
illustration.alt: Yara saying goodbye to her zoo friends as the sun sets, ending her successful detective adventure

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What was special about the banana that went missing?
choices: It was the biggest banana ever grown at the zoo
It was purple instead of yellow
It could talk to the animals
questions.explanation: The zoo had been growing this prize banana for six months, and it was as long as a baseball bat!

questions.question: What kind of footprints did Yara find?
questions.explanation: The footprints were tiny and round, which helped Yara figure out they belonged to guinea pigs.

questions.question: Why did the guinea pigs take the banana?
choices: They were hungry and wanted a snack
They wanted to help their sick friend Pip
They thought it would be fun to play with
questions.explanation: The guinea pigs took the banana because Pip was sick and needed soft food to eat.

questions.question: How did Zookeeper Maria react when she learned the truth?
choices: She was very angry at the guinea pigs
She was happy they helped their friend
She decided to close the zoo
questions.explanation: Maria was touched that the guinea pigs cared so much about helping Pip feel better.

questions.question: How did the story end?
choices: With a birthday party for Pip
With the guinea pigs getting in trouble
questions.explanation: Everyone celebrated with a birthday party for Pip, sharing banana pieces and having fun together!

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Chapter 1
pages.instructions: Choose the correct word to complete each sentence from the story!

sentences.text: She always wore her bright ____ detective hat.
The banana was as long as a ____ bat.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - Chapter 2
pages.instructions: Help Detective Yara solve the mystery by choosing the right words!

sentences.text: The tiny footprints were round like little ____.
Carlos said he saw ____ animals carrying something yellow.
The animals were round and furry with wiggly ____.

pages.title: Fill in the Missing Words - The Solution
pages.instructions: Complete the story about why the guinea pigs took the banana!

sentences.text: ____ was the smallest guinea pig who had been sick.
The guinea pigs took the banana because bananas are ____.
Everyone celebrated with a ____ party for Pip!
